name: ACLU Foundation
mission: The ACLU seeks to defend and preserve democracy and the individual rights and liberties that the Constitution and laws of the United States guarantee everyone in this country.

theoryOfChange: The ACLU Foundation has a published 'TVTP Grant Program Logic Model' on its website (aclu.org). This document, released on December 15, 2022, is described as a 'Policy Document' that explains the goals and objectives of the TVTP program and how they can be achieved.

all_citations.claim: The ACLU seeks to defend and preserve democracy and individual rights through programs like the Center for Liberty and the Southern Collective.
The organization reported total revenue of $185,146,988 and a program expense ratio of 82.5%.
The foundation offers various donation options for supporters to contribute general charitable gifts.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate overall score due to its robust financial health and efficient operations, though its alignment score is lower because its mission is focused on general civil advocacy rather than specific community-based or religious donor priorities.
areas_for_improvement: Limited alignment with specific religious giving priorities for Muslim donors.
Heavy reliance on long-term advocacy which may lack the immediate, measurable outcomes of direct-service charities.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the organization provides a critical platform for sadaqah-eligible charitable giving toward civil rights [3], its alignment score is medium-low because its broad advocacy mission may not directly match the specific fit or urgency sought by donors looking for traditional faith-based interventions [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates strong operational effectiveness by maintaining a program expense ratio of 82.5% [2]. Its capacity-building efforts are channeled through specialized programs such as the Center for Liberty, the Southern Collective, and the State Supreme Court Initiative [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, Inc. works to safeguard the constitutional rights and individual liberties of all people in the United States through litigation and advocacy.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 82.5% ensures the vast majority of funds support direct advocacy and legal work.
Exceptional financial stability with 40.5 months of working capital available.
Highly efficient fundraising operations, spending only $0.10 to raise every $1.
baseline_narrative.summary: The organization operates as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to defending democracy and preserving the legal guarantees provided by the U.S. Constitution [1]. In the most recent fiscal period, the foundation reported a total revenue of $185,146,988 to support its nationwide legal and educational initiatives [2].

alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment for Muslim donors by highlighting its work on <cite id="18">religious freedom and anti-discrimination cases</cite> that specifically protect Muslim communities.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the ACLUF due to its <cite id="1">106-year track record</cite> and <cite id="15">100% independent board members</cite>. It maintains <cite id="12">Candid's Platinum Seal</cite> for public disclosure.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the foundation could publish more frequent, standardized impact reports detailing the outcomes of its <cite id="14">two currently tracked metrics</cite>.

rich_narrative.headline: The American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, Inc. works to safeguard the constitutional rights and individual liberties of all people in the United States through litigation and advocacy.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value systemic legal reform and want to support a long-standing American institution that protects civil liberties through the court system.
donor_motivations: Want to protect constitutional rights for all people in the US
Value long-term legal advocacy over immediate direct aid
Prefer supporting established organizations with massive scale
Interested in funding high-stakes Supreme Court litigation
giving_considerations: The organization has very high reserves and does not have an immediate funding gap
Impact is measured in legal precedents rather than individual lives saved
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prefer funding direct-service humanitarian relief like food or medicine.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The foundation has a strong, verified theory of change and tracks legal outcomes, though it lacks beneficiary-level cost metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The ACLUF uses litigation, advocacy, and public education to defend and expand democracy and constitutional rights.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: In advocacy, evidence is found in court rulings and legislative changes that protect millions of people simultaneously.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The foundation has massive assets and excessive working capital, suggesting it can sustain operations without urgent new funding.

peer_comparison.differentiator: The ACLUF maintains a <cite id="5">higher program ratio (81.5%)</cite> than the <cite id="11">peer median of 78.7%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses more on hate group monitoring and civil rights in the South.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Combines domestic advocacy with international peace and health programs.

strengths.detail: The foundation maintains <cite id="10">40.5 months of working capital</cite>, allowing it to sustain multi-year legal challenges without immediate funding risks.
With an <cite id="5">82.5% program expense ratio</cite>, the charity exceeds the <cite id="11">78.7% peer median</cite> for advocacy organizations.
The organization holds a <cite id="12">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: The ACLUF's strength lies in its institutional maturity and its ability to fund complex, long-term litigation. By maintaining <cite id="4">over $828 million in assets</cite>, the foundation can act as a permanent watchdog for civil liberties. Its grantmaking strategy is also a key strength, as it provided <cite id="7">funding to 187 domestic recipients</cite> in 2024. This decentralized model ensures that national resources support local legal expertise in critical regions like <cite id="13">Florida and Louisiana</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The American Civil Liberties Union Foundation (ACLUF) is a 501(c)(3) institution that has defended constitutional rights in the United States since its <cite id="1">founding in 1920</cite>. The organization operates as the legal and educational arm of the ACLU, focusing on litigation, public education, and advocacy. In the 2024 fiscal year, the foundation reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $185,146,988</cite>. This represents an <cite id="3">8% decline from the previous year</cite>, following a broader trend of post-election revenue stabilization. Despite this dip, the foundation maintains a massive <cite id="4">$828,604,389 in total assets</cite>, providing a significant financial cushion for long-term legal battles. The organization is highly efficient in its resource allocation, maintaining a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 82.5%</cite>. This ensures that the vast majority of donor funds directly support legal filings and advocacy programs. Their fundraising operations are also notably lean, costing only <cite id="6">$0.10 to raise every $1</cite>. The ACLUF serves as a major grantmaker within its own network, distributing <cite id="7">$68,813,122 in domestic grants</cite> to state-level affiliates. These grants empower local offices, such as the <cite id="8">ACLU Foundation of Texas</cite>, to handle regional civil rights issues. While the foundation does not provide direct humanitarian aid, its work in <cite id="9">Supreme Court litigation</cite> and legislative lobbying creates systemic impact by protecting the rights of marginalized communities across the country.
